name: CloudDataBreach
description: A case template for handling suspected or confirmed cloud data exposure or breach incidents, including validation, evidence preservation, cloud scoping, containment, impact assessment, notification, and post-incident remediation.
author: Zachary Carter
summary: Investigation of a suspected or confirmed cloud data exposure or breach involving unauthorized access, disclosure, misuse, or exfiltration of data in a cloud-hosted service, platform, tenant, or storage environment. Scope, affected data, access path, and impact are under investigation.

tasks.title: Validate and classify cloud breach event
tasks.description: Determine whether the incident involves confirmed unauthorized access, public exposure, excessive permissions, compromised credentials, token or session abuse, application compromise, third-party access, misconfiguration, or a false positive. Distinguish suspected exposure from confirmed breach activity.

tasks.title: Initiate stakeholder and provider coordination
tasks.description: Notify and coordinate with internal stakeholders such as cloud administrators, identity teams, security leadership, legal, privacy, compliance, and management, as well as the relevant cloud provider or SaaS vendor if support or investigation assistance is required.

tasks.title: Identify affected cloud services, identities, and data stores
tasks.description: Determine the affected tenant, subscription, account, project, services, applications, storage locations, databases, mailboxes, identities, roles, and third-party integrations. Document confirmed and suspected scope.

tasks.title: Preserve cloud evidence and logging
tasks.description: Preserve relevant audit logs, sign-in logs, API activity, administrative actions, object access records, IAM changes, OAuth activity, app consent records, storage access logs, snapshots, and any other provider or application telemetry needed for investigation and retention.

tasks.title: Determine exposure, access, modification, and exfiltration scope
tasks.description: Assess whether data was merely exposed, actually accessed, modified, deleted, shared, downloaded, or exfiltrated. Identify affected data types, data sensitivity, likely time range, and suspected actors or access sources.

tasks.title: Identify access path and root cause
tasks.description: Investigate how access occurred, such as misconfiguration, compromised credentials, leaked keys, token abuse, excessive permissions, malicious or risky application consent, exploited cloud application weaknesses, or third-party access pathways.

tasks.title: Contain identities, sessions, applications, and cloud resources
tasks.description: Revoke active sessions, disable or restrict compromised accounts, rotate credentials and keys, remove malicious or excessive permissions, disable risky app integrations, remediate public exposure, restrict access to affected resources, and apply any necessary emergency controls.

tasks.title: Assess business, legal, privacy, and regulatory impact
tasks.description: Evaluate the impact to business operations, data confidentiality, data integrity, customers, contracts, regulated data, notification obligations, and overall organizational risk based on the nature and sensitivity of the affected data.

tasks.title: Remediate misconfigurations, credentials, tokens, keys, and app access
tasks.description: Correct misconfigurations, reduce excessive permissions, rotate secrets and access keys, reset credentials, revoke tokens, review service principals and app registrations, remove unauthorized integrations, and validate that the original access path has been closed.

tasks.title: Assess and execute notification obligations
tasks.description: Determine whether customers, regulators, partners, law enforcement, cyber insurance, or other affected parties must be notified based on legal, contractual, privacy, regulatory, or policy requirements, and document all decisions and communications.

tasks.title: Increase monitoring and validate containment
tasks.description: Increase monitoring on affected identities, applications, storage locations, and cloud resources. Validate that containment actions are effective and confirm that no continued unauthorized access, sharing, or exfiltration is occurring.

tasks.title: Implement cloud security improvements and close gaps
tasks.description: Improve IAM controls, alerting, logging retention, conditional access, key management, application governance, data sharing controls, backup and recovery strategy, and detection coverage based on identified weaknesses and lessons learned.

tasks.title: Document findings and close case
tasks.description: Document the affected services, data types, access path, confirmed impact, containment actions, notifications, remediation steps, and lessons learned. Confirm required actions are complete before closing the case.

notes.title: Initial report
notes.content: Document how the possible cloud breach or exposure was identified, when it was discovered, and why the case was opened.

notes.title: Current status
notes.content: Track the current phase of the investigation, key updates, blockers, and next steps.

notes.title: Decision log
notes.content: Record major decisions, approvals, escalations, and significant events during the investigation.

notes.title: Stakeholders and providers
notes.content: List internal stakeholders, cloud administrators, legal and privacy contacts, and any involved provider or vendor contacts.

notes.title: Affected tenant or account
notes.content: Document the affected tenant, subscription, project, account, or organizational cloud boundary.

notes.title: Affected services and applications
notes.content: List the involved cloud services, SaaS platforms, storage services, databases, mail systems, applications, and workloads.

notes.title: Affected identities and roles
notes.content: Track impacted user accounts, service accounts, roles, permissions, groups, and privileged identities.

notes.title: Third-party applications and integrations
notes.content: Document connected apps, service principals, OAuth grants, integrations, and vendor access relevant to the case.

notes.title: Affected data sets
notes.content: List the data stores, repositories, mailboxes, documents, records, objects, or databases involved in the incident.

notes.title: Data sensitivity
notes.content: Document the sensitivity and classification of the affected data, including regulated, confidential, customer, employee, or operational data.

notes.title: Exposure versus confirmed access
notes.content: Distinguish whether data was only exposed, or whether access, download, modification, deletion, or sharing was confirmed.

notes.title: Exfiltration findings
notes.content: Summarize evidence of downloads, exports, sharing, synchronization, data movement, or suspected exfiltration.

notes.title: Timeline of events
notes.content: Build a defensible timeline of relevant cloud, identity, application, and data-access activity.

notes.title: Authentication and session activity
notes.content: Document sign-ins, session behavior, token activity, MFA events, conditional access results, and suspicious login patterns.

notes.title: Administrative and API activity
notes.content: Summarize administrative changes, API calls, object access, permission changes, configuration changes, and related telemetry.

notes.title: IAM and permission findings
notes.content: Document excessive permissions, role changes, public exposure, app consent issues, or other identity and access management weaknesses.

notes.title: Root cause
notes.content: Summarize the suspected or confirmed access path and root cause of the cloud data breach or exposure.

notes.title: Access restriction actions
notes.content: Document session revocations, account disablement, access restriction changes, emergency controls, and tenant-level containment actions.

notes.title: Credential, key, and token actions
notes.content: Track password resets, token revocations, secret rotation, access key rotation, and certificate or credential replacement.

notes.title: Configuration and sharing fixes
notes.content: Document misconfiguration corrections, sharing restriction changes, public access removal, and application governance changes.

notes.title: Validation of remediation
notes.content: Summarize how remediation was validated and how ongoing unauthorized access was ruled out or monitored.

notes.title: Legal and privacy coordination
notes.content: Track discussions, guidance, and decisions involving legal, privacy, and compliance stakeholders.

notes.title: Provider coordination
notes.content: Document provider or vendor support cases, findings, escalations, and response activities.

notes.title: Notification decisions
notes.content: Record notification obligations, decisions, timelines, recipients, and rationale.

notes.title: Executive updates
notes.content: Document status updates, decision points, and escalations communicated to leadership.

notes.title: Findings summary
notes.content: Provide a concise summary of the incident, confirmed impact, root cause, and investigative conclusions.

notes.title: Control improvements
notes.content: Document recommended improvements to IAM, application governance, monitoring, logging, and cloud security posture.

notes.title: Lessons learned
notes.content: Capture lessons learned, process gaps, detection gaps, and follow-up recommendations.
